facts.text: North Plainfield was first inhabited by the Lenni Lenape, Algonquian‑speaking indigenous peoples, long before European settlers arrived.
Dutch families such as the Vermeule and Cadmus settled the area before the American Revolution, laying early foundations for the community.
North Plainfield Township incorporated in 1872 from portions of larger townships, later becoming a distinct borough.
By the 1880s, North Plainfield’s industrial growth lagged behind neighboring Plainfield, which boasted hat factories, printing presses, and carriage shops.
The 1950s Silk Palace barbershop on Plainfield Avenue, owned by George Clinton, became a hub for Parliament‑Funkadelic members and local musicians.
In 2022, a stretch of Plainfield Avenue was officially renamed Parliament Funkadelic Way to honor the town’s rich musical heritage.
